nativeRange.habitat: Lagoons and coastal/nearshore reefs with sandy or silty bottoms, typically at depths of 1–12 m

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting a wide variety of foods, including meaty items (frozen mysis, brine shrimp), herbivore preparations, Spirulina-based flakes, and dried algae sheets. Tends to graze algae and is also known to pick at small benthic invertebrates in its territory.

summary: A rarely-traded Indo-Pacific damselfish considered one of the more aggressive members of the family; generally advised for experienced keepers who can provide robust, large-bodied tankmates and ample rockwork.
careNotes: The Honey Head Damselfish is commonly considered one of the most aggressive damselfish species and tends to become increasingly territorial with age. It has been reported to harass and injure smaller or more docile tankmates, so it is generally advised to house it only with larger, robust fish that can withstand persistent aggression. A well-established reef aquarium with extensive rockwork, caves, and algae growth areas is recommended, as this species tends to claim and defend a territory in the rockwork. It has been known to pick at small benthic crustaceans, so ornamental shrimp and small crabs may be at risk. Juveniles display two tan vertical stripes on a white body with a distinctive black spot edged in yellow/white on the dorsal fin; the rear stripe may fade as the fish matures into its adult coloration. This species is rarely available in the aquarium trade. Tank size sources showed wide disagreement (30 gal/114 L from LiveAquaria, 75 gal/284 L from Quality Marine, 2000 L from Reeflex); 300 L was chosen as a conservative round figure given this species' high aggression level, adult size up to ~18 cm, and stated need for ample swimming space. Adult size sources also varied: Quality Marine listed ~15 cm, FishBase 18.5 cm, Wikipedia/Reeflex 17–20 cm; the range 10–18 cm reflects averaged data. Temperature range averaged across Quality Marine (22–26°C), FishBase wild data (25–29°C), and Reeflex (22–28°C), yielding 23–28°C.
breedingNotes: Has been reported to breed in captivity; males guard and aerate the eggs following courtship. The species is oviparous with distinct pairing during spawning events. Detailed captive breeding protocols are not well-documented in available aquarium hobby literature.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: LiveAquaria species page confirmed scientific name, care level (easy), temperament (semi-aggressive becoming more aggressive with age), diet, minimum tank size (30 gal), and reef compatibility. The designated primary source URL (collections page) did not list this species; the direct species page was found via search.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Cross-checked all high-stakes fields. Provided adult size (~15 cm), tank size (75 gal/284 L), temperature (72–78°F / 22–26°C), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), alkalinity (8–12 dKH), and behavioral notes including aggression rating as among highest of damselfishes.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed scientific name, family (Pomacentridae), maximum size (18.5 cm TL), wild temperature range (25.4–29.3°C), depth (1–12 m), native distribution (Indo-West Pacific), and habitat (lagoons and coastal reefs in silty areas).

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Additional cross-check: adult size 17–20 cm, temperature 22–28°C, tank size recommendation of 2000 L (likely for long-term group/display housing). Diet confirmed as omnivore including algae, crustaceans, zooplankton, detritus. Sandy substrate requirement noted.

fish.breedingNotes: Males are reported to guard and aerate eggs; captive spawning has been observed but raising larvae to juveniles is considered difficult. Detailed protocols are sparse in hobby literature.
